prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, think of it like the little brain inside your computer. It’s that tiny chip where all the thinking happens—like when you add up numbers or decide what to do next. It reads your letters and shows your pictures, doing its work very, very fast. Just like how Grandma follows a recipe step-by-step to bake your favorite cookies, the CPU follows all the instructions to make your games and stories come alive on the screen. It’s the heart of the whole thing, really, quietly humming away to help you.
